summary.intent: Implement missing Microsoft Teams channel features in the moltis codebase by comparing against the openclaw reference implementation, then improve onboarding UX for Teams setup.
summary.outcome: Full Teams channel feature parity achieved: JWT auth, Adaptive Cards, streaming, retries, Graph API tools (search/pins/member-info/reactions), per-team config overrides, agent tools wired to Graph API, and Tailscale Funnel integration in the setup flow. PR #529 created and merged with main.

repo: msteams crate lives at crates/msteams/ with modules: lib.rs, config.rs, activity.rs, state.rs, outbound.rs, plugin.rs, auth.rs, channel_webhook_verifier.rs
Gateway agent tools are registered in crates/gateway/src/server.rs after SendMessageTool; new tool modules go in crates/gateway/src/
Channel descriptor capabilities (supports_interactive, supports_threads, supports_reactions) are declared in crates/channels/src/plugin.rs

RedactedConfig requires a hardcoded field count that must be manually kept in sync with the struct — msteams had count=9 but actually had 14 always-present fields, which would cause runtime serialization panics
Default offered channels list is in crates/config/src/schema.rs and has accompanying tests that check the exact list
Tailscale status is fetched at onboarding startup and available as data.tailscale; public access API is at /api/tailscale/status and /api/tailscale/configure
std::sync::RwLockWriteGuard is not Send and cannot be held across .await points — must scope guards to drop before any async call
Config validation in validate.rs uses Map(Box::new(Leaf)) for msteams which accepts any keys; actual validation happens via serde deserialization into typed structs
Teams requires a public HTTPS endpoint with no polling/socket-mode alternative; Graph change notifications via Azure Event Hub exist but are read-only observer mode only

code.finding: RedactedConfig had hardcoded field count=9 vs actual 14 always-present fields — runtime serialization bug fixed to count=14 with conditional serialization for optional fields
Teams webhook handler now returns 202 Accepted immediately and processes in tokio::spawn background task; requires cloning the Arc before spawn to avoid Send bounds issues
welcomed_conversations uses std::sync::RwLock; guards must be scoped to drop before any .await to satisfy Send requirement for tokio::spawn
Agent tools hold Arc<RwLock<MsTeamsPlugin>> and use account_id param or fall back to first configured account; Graph calls go through MsTeamsPlugin::graph_client() helper
Default offered channels list has tests checking exact contents — must update tests when adding msteams to defaults

workflow: When std::sync::RwLockWriteGuard is not Send, scope the guard in a block before any async call rather than switching to tokio::sync::RwLock
Dead code warnings on new pub(crate) API methods that aren't yet called should use #[allow(dead_code)] rather than removing the methods
Running cargo check -p <crate> is faster than workspace check for iterating on compilation errors in a specific crate
Clippy catches collapsible_if, field_reassign_with_default, and unwrap_in_tests — delegate bulk clippy fixes to a subagent to avoid context pollution
Teams webhooks have ~15s timeout before retry — returning 202 immediately and processing async prevents duplicate messages from Teams retries

friction: Multiple compilation iteration cycles needed: pub(crate) visibility, Send bounds for tokio::spawn, RwLockWriteGuard held across await, dead_code warnings on new API methods
Chunking test failure after implementation — test expected 'word1 word2' at max_len=11 but rfind(' ') returned wrong split point
RedactedConfig field count bug was silent — no compiler error, would only panic at runtime when serializing channel config
Clippy generated many warnings requiring a subagent pass to fix efficiently without polluting main context
JS biome linting required after each web UI edit to catch issues
open_items: Localization of Teams UI strings into fr/zh locale files — all channels have this gap, needs cross-cutting fix
E2E Playwright tests for Teams channel setup flow — no channel currently has dedicated e2e setup tests
Wiring poll vote tracking with persistence (in-memory store only for now)
Graph API permissions documentation for each agent tool (Chat.Read.All, User.Read.All, Chat.ReadWrite.All) — users need to grant these in Azure AD
Per-team config overrides UI in the edit channel modal — backend supports teams.<teamId>.channels.<channelId> nesting but no UI to configure it
